index a1b2c3d..e5f6g7h 100644
--- a/app/api/routes/health.py
+++ b/app/api/routes/health.py
@@ -7,12 +7,16 @@
 from __future__ import annotations
 
 import httpx
+import time
+from datetime import datetime
 
 from fastapi import APIRouter
 
 from app.core.config import get_settings
 from app.core.logging import get_logger
 from app.rag.vector_store import ChromaStore
+
+START_TIME = time.time()
 
 logger = get_logger(__name__)
 router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/health", tags=["health"])
@@ -47,11 +51,14 @@
         logger.warning("ChromaDB health check failed: %s", exc)
 
     status = "ok" if (ollama_ok and chroma_ok) else "degraded"
+    uptime_seconds = int(time.time() - START_TIME)
+    
     return {
         "status": status,
         "ollama": ollama_ok,
         "chromadb": chroma_ok,
         "llm_model": settings.OLLAMA_LLM_MODEL,
         "embed_model": settings.OLLAMA_EMBED_MODEL,
+        "uptime": f"{uptime_seconds}s",
+        "server_time": datetime.now().isoformat(),
     }

scenarios.title: Health check returns ok status when all dependencies are healthy
scenarios.description: Verify that GET /api/health returns status 'ok' when both Ollama and ChromaDB are reachable and functioning correctly.
preconditions: Ollama service is running and accessible at OLLAMA_BASE_URL
ChromaDB is initialized and collection is accessible
scenarios.expected_result: Response contains status='ok', ollama=true, chromadb=true, and llm_model is populated

scenarios.title: Health check returns LLM model configuration in response
scenarios.description: Verify that the health endpoint returns the configured LLM model name from settings.
preconditions: OLLAMA_LLM_MODEL is configured in application settings
scenarios.expected_result: Response includes llm_model field with correct model name from configuration

scenarios.title: Health check returns degraded status when Ollama is unreachable
scenarios.description: Verify that the health endpoint returns 'degraded' status and ollama=false when Ollama HTTP API is not accessible.
preconditions: Ollama service is stopped or OLLAMA_BASE_URL is unreachable
scenarios.expected_result: Response contains status='degraded', ollama=false, chromadb=true; request completes without error

scenarios.title: Health check returns degraded status when ChromaDB fails initialization
scenarios.description: Verify that the health endpoint returns 'degraded' status and chromadb=false when ChromaDB collection cannot be initialized.
preconditions: ChromaDB is misconfigured or collection is inaccessible
scenarios.expected_result: Response contains status='degraded', ollama=true, chromadb=false; request completes without error

scenarios.title: Health check handles Ollama connection timeout gracefully
scenarios.description: Verify that the 3-second timeout for Ollama health check is enforced and doesn't cause request to hang.
preconditions: Ollama endpoint exists but responds slowly (>3 seconds)
scenarios.expected_result: Request completes within 5 seconds total, returns degraded status with ollama=false

scenarios.title: Health check returns degraded when both dependencies fail
scenarios.description: Verify graceful handling when both Ollama and ChromaDB are unavailable simultaneously.
scenarios.expected_result: Response contains status='degraded', ollama=false, chromadb=false; endpoint still returns 200 OK

scenarios.title: Health endpoint handles concurrent requests correctly
scenarios.description: Verify that the health endpoint handles multiple simultaneous requests without race conditions or resource exhaustion.
preconditions: Both Ollama and ChromaDB services are running
scenarios.expected_result: All 10 requests return 200 status with consistent response structure; no timeouts or errors

scenarios.title: Health check does not expose sensitive configuration details
scenarios.description: Verify that the health endpoint only returns necessary information and does not leak sensitive data like API keys or internal URLs.
preconditions: Application has various sensitive settings configured

steps.assertion: only contains: status, ollama, chromadb, llm_model

scenarios.expected_result: Response contains only expected fields (status, ollama, chromadb, llm_model); no sensitive configuration exposed

scenarios.title: Health endpoint is accessible without authentication
scenarios.description: Verify that health endpoint is intentionally public for Kubernetes readiness probes without requiring authentication.
scenarios.expected_result: Health endpoint returns 200 without authentication; intended public access for health probes

scenarios.title: Health check handles non-200 Ollama response correctly
scenarios.description: Verify that health check correctly identifies Ollama as unhealthy when it returns non-200 status codes.
preconditions: Ollama endpoint is reachable but returns error status (500, 404, etc.)
scenarios.expected_result: Health check returns degraded status with ollama=false when Ollama returns non-200 response

generated_specs.content:
"""
Health API endpoint tests for GET /api/health.

Tests cover:
- Happy path scenarios
- Negative scenarios (dependency failures)
- Edge cases (timeouts, concurrent requests)
- Security considerations
"""

import os
import time
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright, APIRequestContext

BASE_URL = os.environ.get("BASE_URL", "http://localhost:8000")


@pytest.fixture
def api_request() -> APIRequestContext:
    """Create an API request context for each test."""
    with sync_playwright() as p:
        context = p.request.new_context(base_url=BASE_URL)
        yield context
        context.dispose()


# SCENARIO_ID: 35cf1c67-f250-41d8-9e0b-08b77cf069cc
@pytest.mark.health
@pytest.mark.api
@pytest.mark.readiness
@pytest.mark.happy_path
def test_health_check_returns_ok_status_when_all_dependencies_healthy(api_request: APIRequestContext):
    """
    Verify that GET /api/health returns status 'ok' when both 
    Ollama and ChromaDB are reachable and functioning correctly.
    
    Preconditions:
    - Ollama service is running and accessible at OLLAMA_BASE_URL
    - ChromaDB is initialized and collection is accessible
    """
    response = api_request.get("/api/health")
    
    assert response.status == 200, f"Expected status 200, got {response.status}"
    
    body = response.json()
    assert body["status"] == "ok", f"Expected status 'ok', got {body['status']}"
    assert body["ollama"] is True, f"Expected ollama=True, got {body['ollama']}"
    assert body["chromadb"] is True, f"Expected chromadb=True, got {body['chromadb']}"
    assert "llm_model" in body, "llm_model field missing from response"
    assert body["llm_model"], "llm_model should be populated"


# SCENARIO_ID: 16742cdb-426c-41e8-a9e7-87d4cfd3b6b0
@pytest.mark.health
@pytest.mark.api
@pytest.mark.configuration
@pytest.mark.happy_path
def test_health_check_returns_llm_model_configuration_in_response(api_request: APIRequestContext):
    """
    Verify that the health endpoint returns the configured LLM model name from settings.
    
    Preconditions:
    - OLLAMA_LLM_MODEL is configured in application settings
    """
    response = api_request.get("/api/health")
    
    assert response.status == 200, f"Expected status 200, got {response.status}"
    
    body = response.json()
    assert "llm_model" in body, "llm_model field missing from response"
    
    expected_model = os.environ.get("OLLAMA_LLM_MODEL", "llama3.2")
    assert body["llm_model"] == expected_model, \
        f"Expected llm_model='{expected_model}', got '{body['llm_model']}'"


# SCENARIO_ID: af3b89c5-6366-4788-96c6-031f2bb97154
@pytest.mark.health
@pytest.mark.api
@pytest.mark.dependency_failure
@pytest.mark.negative
def test_health_check_returns_degraded_status_when_ollama_unreachable(api_request: APIRequestContext):
    """
    Verify that the health endpoint returns 'degraded' status and ollama=false 
    when Ollama HTTP API is not accessible.
    
    Preconditions:
    - Ollama service is stopped or OLLAMA_BASE_URL is unreachable
    - ChromaDB is functioning normally
    """
    response = api_request.get("/api/health")
    
    assert response.status == 200, f"Expected status 200, got {response.status}"
    
    body = response.json()
    assert body["status"] == "degraded", f"Expected status 'degraded', got {body['status']}"
    assert body["ollama"] is False, f"Expected ollama=False, got {body['ollama']}"
    assert body["chromadb"] is True, f"Expected chromadb=True, got {body['chromadb']}"


# SCENARIO_ID: 71f958a4-4466-4a8e-a7b8-b7593cd67cf5
@pytest.mark.health
@pytest.mark.api
@pytest.mark.dependency_failure
@pytest.mark.negative
def test_health_check_returns_degraded_status_when_chromadb_fails_initialization(api_request: APIRequestContext):
    """
    Verify that the health endpoint returns 'degraded' status and chromadb=false 
    when ChromaDB collection cannot be initialized.
    
    Preconditions:
    - Ollama service is running
    - ChromaDB is misconfigured or collection is inaccessible
    """
    response = api_request.get("/api/health")
    
    assert response.status == 200, f"Expected status 200, got {response.status}"
    
    body = response.json()
    assert body["status"] == "degraded", f"Expected status 'degraded', got {body['status']}"
    assert body["ollama"] is True, f"Expected ollama=True, got {body['ollama']}"
    assert body["chromadb"] is False, f"Expected chromadb=False, got {body['chromadb']}"


# SCENARIO_ID: 75e22ef6-9164-4eb0-b24c-24f10767bb62
@pytest.mark.health
@pytest.mark.api
@pytest.mark.timeout
@pytest.mark.performance
@pytest.mark.edge_case
def test_health_check_handles_ollama_connection_timeout_gracefully(api_request: APIRequestContext):
    """
    Verify that the 3-second timeout for Ollama health check is enforced 
    and doesn't cause request to hang.
    
    Preconditions:
    - Ollama endpoint exists but responds slowly (>3 seconds)
    - ChromaDB is functioning
    """
    start_time = time.time()
    response = api_request.get("/api/health")
    elapsed_time = time.time() - start_time
    
    assert elapsed_time < 5, f"Response took {elapsed_time:.2f}s, expected < 5s total"
    assert response.status == 200, f"Expected status 200, got {response.status}"
    
    body = response.json()
    assert body["ollama"] is False, f"Expected ollama=False, got {body['ollama']}"
    assert body["status"] == "degraded", f"Expected status 'degraded', got {body['status']}"


# SCENARIO_ID: 8f15cd8f-c9be-4cb3-a827-3b21b2b08816
@pytest.mark.health
@pytest.mark.api
@pytest.mark.dependency_failure
@pytest.mark.negative
def test_health_check_returns_degraded_when_both_dependencies_fail(api_request: APIRequestContext):
    """
    Verify graceful handling when both Ollama and ChromaDB are unavailable simultaneously.
    
    Preconditions:
    - Ollama service is unavailable
    - ChromaDB is inaccessible
    """
    response = api_request.get("/api/health")
    
    assert response.status == 200, f"Expected status 200, got {response.status}"
    
    body = response.json()
    assert body["status"] == "degraded", f"Expected status 'degraded', got {body['status']}"
    assert body["ollama"] is False, f"Expected ollama=False, got {body['ollama']}"
    assert body["chromadb"] is False, f"Expected chromadb=False, got {body['chromadb']}"


# SCENARIO_ID: 45617072-b9cc-44e0-8b21-d4da0ef5524b
@pytest.mark.health
@pytest.mark.api
@pytest.mark.concurrency
@pytest.mark.load
@pytest.mark.edge_case
def test_health_endpoint_handles_concurrent_requests_correctly():
    """
    Verify that the health endpoint handles multiple simultaneous requests 
    without race conditions or resource exhaustion.
    
    Preconditions:
    - Both Ollama and ChromaDB services are running
    """
    import concurrent.futures
    
    def make_health_request():
        """Make a single health check request."""
        with sync_playwright() as p:
            ctx = p.request.new_context(base_url=BASE_URL)
            resp = ctx.get("/api/health")
            result = {"status": resp.status, "body": resp.json()}
            ctx.dispose()
            return result
    
    concurrent_requests = 10
    
    with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=concurrent_requests) as executor:
        futures = [executor.submit(make_health_request) for _ in range(concurrent_requests)]
        results = [f.result() for f in concurrent.futures.as_completed(futures)]
    
    for i, result in enumerate(results):
        assert result["status"] == 200, \
            f"Request {i} returned status {result['status']}, expected 200"
    
    first_body = results[0]["body"]
    expected_keys = set(first_body.keys())
    
    for i, result in enumerate(results[1:], 1):
        actual_keys = set(result["body"].keys())
        assert actual_keys == expected_keys, \
            f"Request {i} has different keys: {actual_keys}, expected {expected_keys}"


# SCENARIO_ID: 24688443-7c13-4ad6-9b6c-a7ba3b167ee2
@pytest.mark.health
@pytest.mark.api
@pytest.mark.security
@pytest.mark.information_disclosure
def test_health_check_does_not_expose_sensitive_configuration_details(api_request: APIRequestContext):
    """
    Verify that the health endpoint only returns necessary information and 
    does not leak sensitive data like API keys or internal URLs.
    
    Preconditions:
    - Application has various sensitive settings configured
    """
    response = api_request.get("/api/health")
    
    assert response.status == 200, f"Expected status 200, got {response.status}"
    
    body = response.json()
    body_str = str(body)
    
    assert "OLLAMA_BASE_URL" not in body, "OLLAMA_BASE_URL should not be exposed in response"
    assert "REPORTS_DIR" not in body, "REPORTS_DIR should not be exposed in response"
    
    sensitive_terms = ["api_key", "password", "secret", "token", "credential"]
    for term in sensitive_terms:
        assert term.lower() not in body_str.lower(), \
            f"Sensitive term '{term}' should not appear in response"
    
    expected_keys = {"status", "ollama", "chromadb", "llm_model"}
    actual_keys = set(body.keys())
    assert actual_keys == expected_keys, \
        f"Expected keys {expected_keys}, got {actual_keys}"


# SCENARIO_ID: cda75e4c-51a2-4579-86a7-12171e66f6eb
@pytest.mark.health
@pytest.mark.api
@pytest.mark.security
@pytest.mark.authentication
def test_health_endpoint_is_accessible_without_authentication(api_request: APIRequestContext):
    """
    Verify that health endpoint is intentionally public for Kubernetes 
    readiness probes without requiring authentication.
    
    Preconditions:
    - No authentication token provided
    """
    response = api_request.get("/api/health")
    
    assert response.status == 200, \
        "Health endpoint should be accessible without authentication"
    
    body = response.json()
    assert "status" in body, "Response should contain status field"
    assert body["status"] in ["ok", "degraded"], \
        f"Status should be 'ok' or 'degraded', got '{body['status']}'"


# SCENARIO_ID: ad53ec6b-8347-4a82-b090-4e3f2983823c
@pytest.mark.health
@pytest.mark.api
@pytest.mark.error_handling
@pytest.mark.edge_case
def test_health_check_handles_non_200_ollama_response_correctly(api_request: APIRequestContext):
    """
    Verify that health check correctly identifies Ollama as unhealthy 
    when it returns non-200 status codes.
    
    Preconditions:
    - Ollama endpoint is reachable but returns error status (500, 404, etc.)
    """
    response = api_request.get("/api/health")
    
    assert response.status == 200, f"Expected status 200, got {response.status}"
    
    body = response.json()
    assert body["ollama"] is False, f"Expected ollama=False, got {body['ollama']}"
    assert body["status"] == "degraded", f"Expected status 'degraded', got {body['status']}"
